user_message: Summarize what happened with Coho Winery and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 10:44 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 17:53 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Coho Winery

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 12:53 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery QBR preparation

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-02-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:28:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:31:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
